headlines.title: Russia Scrambles for Asian Jet Fuel as Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Russia's fuel crisis has reached the point where one of the world's biggest oil producers is preparing to import jet fuel. According to Reuters, Russia is set to receive at least 200,000 barrels of jet fuel originating in Japan through a complex chain of traders and ship-to-ship

headlines.title: DOJ Urges States to Enforce Fuel Price-Gouging Laws
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is taking its campaign against high gasoline prices to the states, and it’s doing it formally. headlines.title: Oil Markets Grow Numb to U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Drama
headlines.summary: Oil prices remain stuck in a tight range as traders look past geopolitical headlines and wait for clarity on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire. headlines.title: TotalEnergies Offers Millions of Barrels of Iraqi Crude to Asian Buyers
headlines.summary: TotalEnergies is offering millions of barrels of Iraq’s Basrah Medium and Basrah Heavy crudes for prompt delivery to Asia this month and next, traders who had received some of these offers told Bloomberg on Friday. headlines.title: Japan's Top Oil Refiner Eyes a Post-Hormuz Future
headlines.summary: Japan’s biggest oil refiner, Eneos Holdings, will likely move to diversify its crude supply from a heavy dependence on the Middle East in the wake of the shock disruption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz, the company’s chief financial officer, Soichiro Tanaka, told Reuters i

headlines.title: Soaring Imports Push India’s Crude Stocks to Near 1-Year High
headlines.summary: India’s strategic and commercial crude oil inventories have jumped to a nearly one-year high as the world’s third-largest crude oil buyer boosted its imports to a record high in June. headlines.title: Citi: Oil Could Sink to $60 as Hormuz Traffic Normalizes
headlines.summary: Brent Crude prices could plunge to as low as $60 per barrel by the end of the year, says Citigroup, which expects flows through the Strait of Hormuz to soon normalize and the U.S. and Iran to reach a deal in the coming months. “We continue to recommend selling any summer rallies

headlines.title: India Denies Directly Exporting Fuel to Russia, But Admits Traders May Be
headlines.summary: Indian refiners are not directly exporting any refined petroleum products to fuel-starved Russia, although some supplies from traders are likely reaching Russia, India’s Oil Minister, Hardeep Singh Puri, has said. headlines.title: Oil Set for Fourth Straight Weekly Loss as Hormuz Flows Return
headlines.summary: Oil futures were on track to post their fourth consecutive weekly loss early on Friday as the tentative reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the uptick in oil flows weigh down prices. headlines.title: Japan's LNG Imports Fall 7% as Utilities Chase Cheaper Coal
headlines.summary: Japan last month reduced gas-fired generation in favor of coal as the price of liquefied natural gas remained elevated despite multiple reports of energy flows out of the Strait of Hormuz improving.
